items.title: Petronas buyout of PPC stake seen lifting PetChem's earnings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd (PetChem) could benefit majorly if its parent, Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), emerges as the buyer of its 50 per cent stake in Pengerang Petrochemical Company Sdn Bhd (PPC), a move analysts say could remove a major earnings drag while unlocking value for shareholders. While Petronas, which owns a 64.4 per cent stake in PetChem, has yet to confirm whether it will also acquire PetChem's interest in PPC, HLIB believes such a move would offer compelling strategic and financial benefits. HLIB analyst Thye May Ting said full ownership of PPC would allow Petronas to integrate upstream refining and downstream petrochemical operations at Pengerang under a single entity.

items.title: Three days trapped in Gunung Jerai ravine, desperate cries save solo hiker
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — A solo hiker who spent nearly three days trapped in a ravine on Gunung Jerai in Kedah was rescued after three passing hikers heard his desperate cries for help and alerted rescuers, according to Berita Harian . He lost all of his personal belongings in the fall, leaving him unable to call for help. His ordeal ended last night when three hikers heard him shouting from about 15m to 20m below the cliff edge and contacted the Kedah Fire and Rescue Department.

items.title: Weststar-Rocket Technologies targets over RM42bil in aerospace, defence opportunities
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Weststar Group and Australia's Rocket Technologies International Pty Ltd (RTI) have formed a strategic joint venture (JV) expected to unlock commercial opportunities exceeding RM42 billion through future aerospace, defence, satellite and advanced technology programmes in Malaysia and Australia. The partnership, through Weststar Defence Industries Sdn Bhd, will see the two companies establish JV entities in Malaysia and Australia to develop sovereign aerospace and defence capabilities, deepening bilateral cooperation in advanced technologies while supporting long-term industrial and economic growth. The signing was witnessed by Tan Sri Dr Johari Abdul, Speaker of Malaysia's Dewan Rakyat; Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, Malaysia's Defence Minister; and Datin Paduka Sharrina Abdullah, Malaysia's High Commissioner to Australia.

items.title: In Dewan Rakyat today: Focus on Suhakam Act amendments, cost of living and Cybercrime Bill
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — Proposed amendments to the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) Act 1999 and the gap between the inflation rate and the cost of living are among the key focus in the Dewan Rakyat today. According to the Order Paper on Parliament’s official website, during the question-and-answer session, Teresa Kok Suh Sim (PH-Seputeh) will ask the Prime Minister whether the government plans to amend the Suhakam Act to allow the commission to generate additional income through training programmes and courses, so that it will no longer be fully dependent on government allocations. During the same session, V. Ganabatirau (PH-Klang) will ask the Prime Minister on the status of the review of land transfers or changes in land use involving flood retention ponds, particularly in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: More than 1,000 schools nationwide deemed structurally unsafe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: More than 1,000 schools nationwide have been deemed structurally unsafe, with the Education Ministry continuing efforts to replace the buildings in phases. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said that as of the first quarter of 2026, a total of 1,061 schools had buildings categorised under Scale 7, the classification used for dilapidated structures. Fadhlina said state education departments continuously assessed the need and feasibility of constructing replacement school buildings for schools categorised under Scale 7.

items.title: Microsoft to cut under 2.5pct of workforce in latest layoffs, Business Insider reports
items.summary: BENGALURU: Microsoft is planning to cut fewer than 2.5 per cent of its workforce in its latest round of layoffs, which could be announced as early as next week, Business Insider reported on Tuesday, citing sources. Reuters could not immediately verify the report. Here are a few details: * The layoffs will affect thousands of roles, including positions in sales and consulting, as well as jobs in the Xbox gaming division, the *Business Insider* report said.

items.title: Data centre power demand in Peninsular Malaysia to surge to 31pct by 2035
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Electricity consumption by data centres in Peninsular Malaysia is projected to surge from seven per cent of total demand in 2026 to 31 per cent by 2035, Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said. He said the government, through the Energy Commission, was closely monitoring rising electricity demand, particularly in Peninsular Malaysia, to ensure the country's power supply remains secure and reliable. Fadillah said the rapid growth of artificial intelligence (AI) and data centre industries would also drive a sharp increase in peak electricity demand.

items.title: Cybercrimes Bill to replace Computer Crimes Act, says NSC
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 1 —The Cybercrimes Bill 2026 is not merely intended to comply with the Council of Europe Convention on Cybercrime and the United Nations Convention against Cybercrime, but also to provide for criminal offences under Parts III to VI of the Bill, as well as offences under any other written law involving the use of computer systems. The National Security Council (NSC) said in a statement yesterday that the Bill had been drafted based on the suitability of Malaysia’s existing legal framework and law enforcement mechanisms. “In addition, the NSC, through the National Cyber Security Agency, also gave a briefing on the Cybercrimes Bill 2026 to the 15th Parliament’s Special Select Committee on Security and the Special Select Committee on Infrastructure, Transport and Communications on Feb 25, 2026,” the statement said.

items.title: Boy, 6, found dead in Perak plantation drain hours after vanishing from home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — A desperate search for a six-year-old boy who vanished from his home ended in tragedy after he was found dead in a drain at a nearby oil palm plantation in Perak. Firefighters, police, the Malaysian Civil Defence Force and members of the public launched an extensive search that stretched into the nearby plantation, with police deploying a K9 sniffer dog to help comb the area. Sabarodzi said firefighters worked alongside police, Civil Defence personnel and local residents, while the police K9 unit searched the oil palm plantation behind the family’s home.

items.title: Missing hiker found safe after three days in ravine
items.summary: The man had been missing since Sunday after he went hiking alone at 4pm and slipped into the ravine. The Kedah Fire and Rescue Department operations centre said it received a distress call at 7.30pm. Senior Operations Commander Deputy Fire Superintendent II Ahmad Naufal Abdullah also arrived shortly after.

items.title: Government to restructure foreign worker management, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 —The government will restructure foreign worker management in the country to ensure it’s more coordinated, efficient and aligned with industry needs, says Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. According to Ahmad Zahid, the meeting reached several key decisions, including matters related to the membership and terms of reference of the Cabinet Committee on Foreign Workers. Ahmad Zahid said the government would also review foreign labour requirements more strategically, based on the actual needs of industries.

items.title: Malaysia household debt hits RM1.73t as burden eases to 84.4pc of GDP, says Anwar
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said household debt stood at 84.4 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP) at the end of the first quarter, down marginally from 84.7 per cent three months earlier. In a written parliamentary reply, Anwar, who is also finance minister, said the government and Bank Negara Malaysia continued to monitor household indebtedness, repayment capacity and broader economic conditions to ensure families could weather potential external shocks. Anwar said the median debt service ratio for outstanding household loans stood at 33 per cent, while the median debt-to-income ratio remained unchanged at 1.3 times, suggesting debt growth continued to be supported by rising incomes rather than excessive borrowing.

items.title: National Education Council formed to reshape Malaysia's education system, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The National Education Council has been established to drive comprehensive reforms in the country’s education system to ensure it remains relevant to current demands, including through the improvement of content and curriculum, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said this after chairing the National Education Council Meeting No. 1 of 2026, which examined the direction of national education covering both school education and higher learning.

items.title: #NSTviral: Shoppers go bananas over monkey business at Sungai Petani grocery store
items.summary: The monkey was seen climbing a beam near the ceiling, turning an otherwise routine grocery run into a frenzied scene. In a video posted by Instagram user @madhilmie_, shoppers and workers were seen reacting frantically as the monkey perched above them. User @ayampenyetadilia said the monkey caused a stir while people were doing their morning shopping.

items.title: South Korea exports post strongest growth since 1978
items.summary: Petroleum product exports increased 49.8 per cent on higher oil prices. By destination, shipments to China and the US rose 92.1 per cent and 78.6 per cent, respectively, while exports to the European Union increased 31.8 per cent. Imports rose 30.1 per cent to US$66.10 billion, after increasing 20.7 per cent in the previous month.

items.title: Motac seeks diesel usage data before deciding aid for tour bus operators
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The Ministry of Tourism, Arts and Culture (Motac) has urged tour bus operators and tourism associations that have yet to submit diesel usage data and related documents to do so immediately, to enable the government to assess appropriate forms of assistance following the rise in diesel price. According to Motac, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) has in principle agreed to consider proposals for assistance to tour bus operators, but the decision must be supported by complete and accurate data. “Motac has requested the relevant associations and operators to submit diesel usage data, as well as comparisons of fuel costs before and after the Middle East crisis.

items.title: Are all EV prices going to rise from today? Here’s what the new price rules on ‘fully imported’ EVs mean for the industry
items.summary: With the introduction of a minimum cost, insurance and freight value of RM200,000 per imported EV unit, fully imported electric vehicles are expected to see a significant price increase. Proton Origin: Malaysia Proton is expanding its lineup with EVs, focusing on homegrown models including Proton eMas 7 and eMas 5. Perodua Origin: Malaysia Perodua is also venturing into the EV market with its QV-E model.

items.title: Middle East conflict drags global air travel demand lower in May, IATA says
items.summary: IATA said total passenger demand, measured in revenue passenger kilometres (RPK), declined 2.2 per cent year-on-year (YoY) in May 2026, while available seat kilometres (ASK) fell 2.3 per cent. International passenger traffic slipped 1.6 per cent from a year earlier, while capacity contracted 2.4 per cent. Domestic travel was weaker, with demand falling 3.1 per cent and capacity down 2.1 per cent, resulting in a load factor of 83 per cent.

items.title: Trump earned over US$1bil from crypto ventures in 2025
items.summary: NEW YORK: President Donald Trump recorded around US$1.2 billion in income from his cryptocurrency activities in 2025, according to documents released Tuesday by the US Office of Government Ethics. World Liberty Financial issued its own cryptocurrency, WLFI, whose initial sale brought in US$550 million. Trump and his three sons also obtained, via an intermediary company, DT Marks Defi, an additional 22.5 billion WLFI, currently worth around US$1.3 billion.

items.title: Malaysia rolls out new passport with 94 security features, 10-year option
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 1 — The security features of the Malaysian International Passport will continue to be enhanced periodically to ensure that the travel document, currently ranked as the third most powerful passport in the world, remains secure, difficult to forge and compliant with international standards. Immigration Department director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said that although no cases of physical forgery of Malaysian passport booklets have been detected so far, the passport’s security features must be updated regularly to prevent existing security measures from becoming known to irresponsible parties. He said Malaysia last introduced a new passport design in 2016, followed by minor security enhancements in 2023.

items.title: Nike flags prolonged turnaround as China slump, weak outlook eclipse quarterly beat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Nike signalled on Tuesday that chief executive officer Elliott Hill's turnaround strategy still faces significant obstacles, as persistent weakness in China and a cautious outlook overshadowed a modest fourth-quarter revenue beat. Quarterly results, which include a 1.0 per cent decline in revenue and a double-digit sales decline in China, failed to reassure investors seeking clear signs that Hill's nearly two-year effort to revive growth is gaining traction. Nike's shares have fallen 35 per cent so far this year.

items.title: Household debt stands at RM1.73 trillion, 84.4pct of GDP, Anwar says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's household debt stood at RM1.73 trillion at the end of March 2026, equivalent to 84.4 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP), Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said indicators of household repayment capacity remained strong, with the median debt service ratio (DSR) for outstanding household loans standing at 33 per cent. Anwar said the government remained committed to ensuring household debt growth remained under control and in line with borrowers' repayment capacity.

items.title: Ringgit opens firmer against greenback as US posts weaker consumer confidence data
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit was firmer against the US dollar in the early session, supported by weaker-than-expected US consumer confidence data that weighed on the greenback. Meanwhile, investors remained cautious ahead of the release of the US nonfarm payrolls report later this week. At 8.01am, the local currency rose to 4.0775/0870 against the greenback from Tuesday's close of 4.0825/0870 Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US Conference Board Consumer Confidence Index came in at 91.2 points in June, well below the consensus estimates of 94.4 points.

items.title: Japan manufacturing extends growth, caps best quarter since Q1 2014, PMI shows
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's manufacturing sector expanded in June, capping its best quarterly performance since early 2014, as new orders grew at their fastest pace in more than two years, a business survey showed on Wednesday. The S&P Global Japan Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index (PMI) rose to 54.8 in June from 54.5 in May, expanding for a sixth consecutive month and broadly in line with the flash reading. Output rose at the second-fastest pace since January 2022, after April this year.

items.title: Starmer boosts budget to modernise UK military before exit
items.summary: MAIDENHEAD, United Kingdom: Britain plans to spend almost £300 billion ($397 billion) over the next four years to modernise its armed forces to counter rising threats, outgoing Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced Tuesday. More drones, uncrewed vehicles and an upgrade to the UK's nuclear deterrent form part of Starmer's long-awaited 10-year Defence Investment Plan (DIP), which he described as his "legacy." Two defence ministers quit earlier this month in a row over the defence plan, including defence secretary John Healey who said it risked making Britain "less safe."

items.title: South Korea factory activity grows at slower pace as export demand weakens, PMI shows
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korea's factory activity expanded in June for the seventh consecutive month, though at a slower pace than the previous month amid weakening export demand, a survey showed on Wednesday. Output and new orders grew more slowly, while new export orders fell for the second consecutive month due to the lingering impact of the Middle East conflict, according to the survey. South Korea's factory output fell for the second consecutive month in May, missing expectations, data showed on Tuesday.

items.title: Outcry over elephant wedding procession in Kelantan prompts Perhilitan task force
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 1 — As animal rights groups slammed the use of elephants in a wedding procession last weekend, the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan) responded late last night by announcing it will set up a special task force to review and improve existing laws and standard operating procedures (SOPs). She said elephants are endangered and held in trust by Perhilitan for conservation. I cannot believe that Perhilitan would turn our majestic elephants into dressed-up props for weddings,” she said.

items.title: World Bank to phase out lending to China by 2031
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The World Bank will phase out its lending to China by 2031, according to the organisation's new country partnership framework, a source familiar with the matter told AFP on Tuesday. "China has made significant development advances over the past several decades, progress that the World Bank and others have supported," said a World Bank official familiar with the matter, speaking on condition of anonymity. "The World Bank's role is shifting from lender to knowledge partner, in line with China's development trajectory," said the World Bank official.

items.title: Missing boy, 6, found dead after search near Perak oil palm plantation
items.summary: BAGAN DATUK: A six-year-old boy reported missing from his home in Sungai Sumun, Hutan Melintang, was found dead in a drain at a nearby oil palm plantation last night. Perak Fire and Rescue Department operations division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received a call from the police at 6.24pm reporting the child's disappearance. "He later lodged a police report at 3.30pm reporting his son's disappearance," Sabarodzi said today.

items.title: Japan business mood improves despite Middle East war, BOJ survey shows
items.summary: The headline index measuring big manufacturers' business sentiment stood at +22 in June, up from +17 in March, and exceeded a median market forecast of +16. It marked the highest level since March 2018. An index gauging big non-manufacturers' sentiment stood at +37, up from +36 in March.

items.title: Three-year-old rescued six days after Venezuela quakes
items.summary: CARACAS: A three-year-old boy was rescued from the rubble in Caracas on Tuesday, a Jordanian rescue team said, six days after twin earthquakes hit Venezuela, killing almost 2,000 people. Video footage showed rescue workers cheering upon discovering the child, who had miraculously survived. The boy received first aid and was immediately taken to hospital, the Jordanian civil defense said in a statement.

items.title: Easier, greener bill management system
items.summary: One simple change can make life easier while supporting a more sustainable future: switching to Air Selangor's e-Bil for easier water bill management. For Air Selangor customers, e-Bil offers a convenient, efficient, and environmental-friendly way to manage water accounts. The Air Selangor app also offers customers greater control over their water usage.

items.title: Businesses back MTEN efforts, but seek greater policy detail
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Business groups welcome government efforts to cushion geopolitical impacts but say lack of details and engagement limits assessment of effectiveness. They cited high costs, weak demand and cash flow pressures, while calling for financing, digitalisation support and regulatory relief. The SME Association of Malaysia national president Dr Chin Chee Seong welcomed the government's proactive approach but said it had yet to receive details on the more than 120 measures approved under the National Economic Action Council (MTEN).

items.title: LiveWire fuels startup growth beyond funding
items.summary: These are among the advantages offered by Shell LiveWire Malaysia, which returns for its 2026 edition with a continued focus on helping entrepreneurs refine their business models, strengthen their ventures and accelerate growth. Founded in 2020, PlusVibes is Malaysia's first on-demand mental health platform connecting users with therapists anonymously while providing digital well-being tools, corporate mental health programmes and community initiatives. While building the business, Madiha came across Shell LiveWire through Malaysia's startup ecosystem.

items.title: Angkasa fast tracks RM200mil Boost stake buy: Sources
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Angkatan Koperasi Kebangsaan Malaysia Bhd (Angkasa) is accelerating plans to buy a RM200 million strategic stake in Boost Holdings Sdn Bhd. Boost is the largest shareholder of Boost Bank Bhd, one of the five digital banks licensed by Bank Negara Malaysia. The investment would give Angkasa a direct entry into the country's fast-growing digital banking sector.

items.title: NASA rolls out more Moon base plans
items.summary: WASHINGTON: NASA on Tuesday announced new uncrewed missions to aid in the future creation of a lunar surface base, a project beginning to take shape despite recent setbacks. Senior agency officials have acknowledged that the situation would likely cause lunar programme delays, but voiced optimism Tuesday, also indicating there were alternatives to launch a lander carrying equipment developed by Blue Origin. They pledged $20 billion to the Moon base efforts.

items.title: Venezuela quake survivors seek food, shelter as toll rises to nearly 2,000
items.summary: LA GUAIRA: Tens of thousands of people urgently need food and shelter in Venezuela after the two devastating earthquakes that killed nearly 2,000, the UN said on Tuesday, as doctors warned of outbreaks of disease with survivors sleeping in the streets. The UN refugee agency said "food shortages are widespread, basic services have broken down and connectivity has been largely severed" in the port city of La Guaira – the worst hit area north of the capital Caracas. The UN agency said it needed some $14.85 million to scale up aid and temporary shelter for 30,000 people over six months.

items.title: 'Alleged access of PM's bank data incredibly concerning'
items.summary: AUSTRALIAN Treasurer Jim Chalmers said yesterday a report that two Ernst & Young (EY) employees on temporary assignment at Commonwealth Bank of Australia (CommBank) allegedly accessed Prime Minister Anthony Albanese's personal banking details was "incredibly concerning". EY declined to comment on the report. The Australian Federal Police said it had charged two Sydney men on May 6 with allegedly accessing restricted personal banking data belonging to a federal parliamentarian.

items.title: The 8 Johor hot seats to watch after Pas' strategic shift
items.summary: BN has fielded MCA's Chua Jian Boon. Without Pas's election machinery this election, observers believe Bersatu faces a daunting task in defending the seat, which will see a three-cornered fight. BN then fielded her in the seat.

items.title: RM100 grocery challenge: What a basic basket gets a Malaysian family in 2026
items.summary: Today, that same RM100 note feels like it stretches a little less with every visit to the supermarket. To find out, Malay Mail conducted a supermarket challenge: Build a balanced grocery basket for a small household using a strict RM100 budget. The result was a mix of fresh essentials — vegetables, eggs, protein, and pantry staples — that illustrates both the resilience of local supply chains and the difficult trade-offs shoppers must now make.

items.title: Security-enhanced passport launched
items.summary: THE new passport being issued from today will have 94 security features that strengthen protections against forgery, alteration and misuse. Immigration director-general Datuk Zakaria Shaaban said yesterday the improvements, nearly double the previous version's 49, included visual, covert and forensic security features. He said Malaysians could get either a 10-year passport for RM350 or the five-year passport for RM200.

items.title: Perak opens first Islamic high school for Orang Asli
items.summary: SUNGAI SIPUT: The establishment of the first Islamic secondary school for the Orang Asli community marks a significant milestone for indigenous students. Sultan of Perak Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah said Sekolah Menengah Agama Rakyat Orang Asli Nurul Hidayah in Kampung Kenang here symbolised the state's commitment to ensuring every child had access to quality education as well as the opportunity to build a better future. "What began as a modest centre for Islamic outreach and 'fardu ain' classes has grown into the first institution in Perak to offer an integrated and standardised curriculum combining academic and Islamic education for the Orang Asli.

items.title: Parliament votes through EIS amendments introducing progressive fines for unreported job vacancies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Dewan Rakyat today approved amendments to the Employment Insurance System (Amendment) Bill 2025, introducing a progressive penalty structure for employers who fail to notify the Social Security Organisation (Socso) of job vacancies. He said guidance and engagement sessions would continue to help employers comply with the reporting requirements. Khairul Firdaus said the amendments also took into account feedback from employers gathered through engagement and advocacy sessions conducted by Socso with various sectors nationwide.

items.title: Perak-born man loses citizenship appeal despite having Malaysian father and passport
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Perak-born Haritharan Mugunthan, 25, today failed again in court to be declared a Malaysian citizen. This is despite him being born to a Malaysian father, and having once been issued a Malaysian passport when he was just more than one year old. The Court of Appeal unanimously decided today that Haritharan had failed to fulfill the Federal Constitution's requirements to be a Malaysian automatically.

items.title: Strong start for Shah Alam LRT3 as Rapid KL reports 60,000 trips on first full day of service
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The newly launched Shah Alam Light Rail Transit Line 3 (LRT3) has hit the ground running, recording more than 60,000 passenger trips on its first full day of service this Monday. The figure, according to operator Rapid KL, covers the entire network ecosystem, including train services, feeder buses, and the Rapid KL On-Demand (ROD) service, providing commuters with a seamless, end-to-end journey. Of the total ridership, 58,865 trips were made via train, while 3,319 were recorded across the first-and-last mile network, comprising 1,883 feeder bus trips and 1,436 ROD journeys.

items.title: MoF cuts unsubsidised fuel prices by 10 sen ahead of BUDI Diesel rollout
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 30 — Retail prices for unsubsidised petrol and diesel will drop by 10 sen per litre for the week of July 1 to July 8, the Ministry of Finance announced today. Unsubsidised RON95 will fall to RM3.37 per litre from RM3.47, while unsubsidised diesel drops to RM3.97 per litre from RM4.07. RON95 under the BUDI95 programme stays at RM1.99 per litre, while diesel under BUDI Diesel remains at RM2.10 per litre.

items.title: Madani book vouchers: 2.2 million students get RM100 each tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — More than 2.2 million students under the Ministry of Education (MOE) will benefit from the MADANI Book Voucher Programme 2026, backed by an allocation of RM221.6 million, with redemption of RM100 e-vouchers beginning tomorrow until Oct 31, the Dewan Rakyat was told today. Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said 2,217,579 recipients comprising students from Remove Class to Form Six, vocational colleges, matriculation colleges and the Malaysian Institute of Teacher Education (IPGM) would benefit from the initiative. Each recipient is required to purchase at least one book from the MADANI Special Title Focus category,” he said during question time.

items.title: Johor BN denies involvement in vandalism of Pakatan campaign materials, calls for probe
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) has denied any involvement in the incident that involved damaging Pakatan Harapan (PH) flags and campaign materials ahead of the state election. Johor BN information chief Md Israk Abdullah said the state coalition has never ordered, encouraged or sanctioned any form of political vandalism. No party should try to take advantage of this incident to tarnish BN’s name without clear evidence.

items.title: From stray dogs to Wi-Fi: Pakatan candidate Guna Balakrishnan lists the top headaches for Layang-Layang voters
items.summary: The Layang-Layang state seat witnessed a three-cornered contest involving Guna, Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Chua Jian Boon and incumbent Abd Mutalip Abd Rahim, representing Perikatan Nasional (PN). Abd Mutalip Abd Rahim (PN-Bersatu) 3. Chua Jian Boon (BN-MCA) (2022: Abd Mutalip Abd Rahim: BN-Umno: Maj: 2,815) — Bernama

items.title: MOH shuts down own website temporarily after cyber scare, says patient data safe
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 30 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has temporarily suspended access to its official website to allow for enhanced cybersecurity measures to be implemented. “The ministry’s healthcare delivery systems continue to operate as normal and are protected by separate infrastructure with strict cybersecurity controls,” it said. On Saturday, media reports said the MOH official portal experienced access disruptions believed to be linked to a cybersecurity incident.

items.title: Army sets up inquiry board, suspends heavy weapons live-fire training after Hobart Camp incident leaves two soldiers hurt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Malaysian Army has formed a board of inquiry to determine the cause of the incident involving two soldiers who were injured during a live-fire training exercise at Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah, yesterday. Army Chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman said all live-fire training involving support weapons has also been temporarily suspended pending the outcome of the investigation, BuletinTV3 reported today. He said the special board will examine three key areas — human factors, technical issues, and compliance with standard operating procedures (SOPs).

items.title: Over nine million STR recipients now eligible for mySalam health payouts, govt mulls extension
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — A total of 9.15 million Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) recipients are eligible for benefits under the MySalam B40 National Protection Scheme based on this year’s criteria. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said that since its launch in 2019 until Dec 31, 2025, some 1.88 million individuals have received payouts totalling RM1.42 billion. With a remaining fund balance of RM490.9 million as of end-2025, the scheme remains well-positioned to continue supporting its target group.

items.title: Man dies after falling from 14th floor apartment in Kuching
items.summary: Berita Harian reported that in the incident at about 2.10pm, the victim was found lodged at the fourth floor of the building before being confirmed dead by Health Ministry (KKM) personnel at the scene. A spokesperson for the Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department Operations Centre said they received an emergency call regarding the incident at 2.15pm. He said a team from the Batu Lintang Fire and Rescue Station was immediately deployed to the location to carry out rescue operations.

items.title: Wet weather alert: Thunderstorms, heavy rain expected in KL, Putrajaya and 11 states until 7pm, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for several states, with heavy rain, lightning and strong winds expected until 7pm today. In a post on Facebook, MetMalaysia said affected areas include Perlis; Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu); Penang (South Seberang Perai); Perak (Kerian); Pahang (Raub, Bentong, Bera and Rompin); Selangor (Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang); the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya; Negeri Sembilan; Melaka; and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Muar and Mersing). In East Malaysia, the warning covers Sarawak’s Samarahan, Betong, Sarikei, Sibu, Mukah, Kapit (Song), Bintulu (Tatau), Miri (Subis) and Limbang, as well as Sabah’s Interior (Sipitang, Tenom and Nabawan), West Coast (Ranau), Tawau (Tawau and Lahad Datu) and Sandakan (Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran and Sandakan).

items.title: Cheaper and faster: How the new LRT3 is changing life at UiTM Shah Alam
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 30 — The operation of the Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) Shah Alam Line has delivered significant benefits to students of Universiti Teknologi MARA (UiTM) Shah Alam by improving daily mobility and helping reduce traffic congestion around the campus area. He was speaking after launching the Semiconductor@UiTM initiative at the Tuanku Abdul Halim Mu’adzam Shah Engineering Complex, UiTM Shah Alam. The Shah Alam Line comprises 20 stations: Bandar Utama, Kayu Ara, BU11 (Bandar Utama 11), Damansara Idaman, Subang, Glenmarie 2, Kerjaya, Stadium Shah Alam, Dato’ Menteri, UiTM Shah Alam, Section 7 Shah Alam, Bandar Baru Klang, Pasar Klang, Jalan Meru, Jambatan Kota, Taman Selatan, Seri Andalas, Klang Jaya, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia.

items.title: Another incident at Hobart Camp, two soldiers injured after weapon explodes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — Two military personnel, a corporal and a sergeant, sustained knee injuries after a firearm used during training at Hobart Camp in Gurun, Kedah, exploded due to a technical malfunction yesterday. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the injuries were confirmed not to have been caused by bullets, but rather by fragments or foreign objects propelled when the weapon suddenly exploded. “Preliminary investigations found that a technical issue occurred when the weapon experienced stoppages or malfunctioned during training.

items.title: Home again: Malaysian Tak Bai bomb survivors now under treatment in Kota Bharu hospital
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 30 — Two Malaysian men who were injured in a bomb explosion in Tak Bai, Narathiwat, southern Thailand yesterday were transferred to Raja Perempuan Zainab II Hospital (HRPZII) in Kota Bharu today for further medical treatment. HRPZII director Dr Izamin Idris said the victims, Abdullah Syarapi Abd Rahman, 45, and Muhammad Yusri Udin, 38, would first undergo assessment at the Emergency Department before receiving subsequent treatment. “The victims arrived at the hospital at 10.50 am today from Narathiwat Ratchanakarin Hospital via the Rantau Panjang Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex.

items.title: UM staff fail in final bid to challenge compulsory Covid-19 vaccination circular
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 30 — A group of Universiti Malaya (UM) staff failed to obtain Federal Court leave to appeal the dismissal of their challenge against the government’s circular requiring compulsory Covid-19 vaccination. On October 27, 2021, the UM staff as well as several teachers from different government schools filed their judicial review application against six respondents, namely the education minister, education director-general, Public Service Department (PSD) director-general, the health minister, health director-general and Malaysian government. The group was initially granted leave by the High Court in May 2022 to commence a judicial review challenging the circular.

items.title: Woman found in suitcase in Kajang apartment died from slash to the head, say cops
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 30 — The woman who was found stuffed inside a suitcase in an apartment in Kajang, Selangor, died after being slashed in the head, police said today. Kajang police chief Naazron Abdul Yusof said the post-mortem was completed yesterday evening and confirmed that the victim’s body was found intact, according to Free Malaysia Today . Police later found the victim stuffed inside a suitcase in the living room.

items.title: Larkin for a comeback: Pakatan candidate Suhaizan Kaiat bets on high turnout to flip the seat
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 30 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Larkin seat, Suhaizan Kaiat, is optimistic about recapturing the seat from Barisan Nasional (BN) in the 16th Johor state election. In GE14, the Larkin seat was won by Datuk Mohd Izhar Ahmad, who contested under the PH (Bersatu) banner, before BN’s Mohd Hairi Mad Shah reclaimed the seat in the 2022 Johor state election. Mohd Hairi Mad Shah (BN-Umno) (2022: Mohd Hairi Mad Shah: BN-Umno: Majority: 6,178) — Bernama
